creator: m.seaman@infracaninophile.co.uk
text:
From the announcement message:

"Welcome to phpMyAdmin 3.4.3.2 and to phpMyAdmin 3.3.10.3, which are
security releases.

Please refer to the upcoming PMASA-2011-9 to PMASA-2011-12 announcements
on http://www.phpmyadmin.net/home_page/security/.

Details will appear on http://phpmyadmin.net. In a hurry? you can visit
http://sourceforge.net/projects/phpmyadmin to download.

Marc Delisle, for the team"

Still awaiting publication ofthe advisories.  The only detail I've
found so far is in the ChangeLog:

3.4.3.2 (2011-07-23)
- [security] Fixed XSS vulnerability, see PMASA-2011-9
- [security] Fixed local file inclusion vulnerability, see PMASA-2011-10
- [security] Fixed local file inclusion vulnerability and code execution, see PMASA-2011-11
- [security] Fixed possible session manipulation in swekey authentication, see PMASA-2011-12
